identity.name: DNS Tunneling Exfiltration — High-Entropy Label Burst (T1048.003 / T1572)
identity.summary: An implant encodes data into the labels of DNS queries to a single attacker-controlled zone, producing a burst of long, high-entropy subdomain (and TXT) lookups from one source. The CortexSim dns_tunnel_exfil EAL plugin chunks a payload into base32/base64 labels and emits A and TXT queries against the authorised testmynids.org zone. NGFW DNS Security Enhanced Application Logs feed XSIAM Network…
identity.description: DNS tunneling is a durable exfiltration and C2 channel because recursive DNS is almost universally allowed outbound, even from segmented networks. Tools such as iodine and dnscat2 (and bespoke implants) encode payload bytes into the leftmost labels of queries to a zone whose authoritative server the attacker controls; responses (often in TXT records) carry data back. The telemetry is entirely network/DNS: NGFW DNS Security logs the query name, type, source and zone into the Enhanced Application Log, and XSIAM Network Security Analytics models three independent signals — a sudden spike in queries-per-source to one zone, an abnormally high character-entropy/base32-base64 label profile, and FQDN lengths far above the host's normal baseline. This card detects the tunnel from panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story using dns_query_name, the query type, the destination zone (dst_host), and per-source aggregation. A correlation rule fuses the volume, entropy and length signals (plus the TXT burst) into one exfiltration incident, and an analytics-plane stitch ties the DNS exfil back to the originating endpoint's process lineage in XSIAM.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1048.003 / T1572 — CortexSim dns_tunnel_exfil EAL plugin (base32/base64-encoded A and TXT label bursts to one zone)

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-NDR-002 requirements (NGFW with DNS Security + EAL forwarding to XSIAM; recursive DNS resolution for testmynids.org permitted).
NGFW forwards DNS Enhanced Application Logs into XSIAM (panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story carries dns_query_name).

payload.code:
# step-1: Inspect DNS tunnel plugin params schema
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli describe dns_tunnel_exfil
# step-2: Launch live DNS tunneling campaign — A queries with high-entropy labels
cat > /tmp/dns-tunnel-live.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-NDR-002
name: "NDR validation — DNS tunnel live"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - testmynids.org
dry_run: false
tags: [ndr, dns-tunnel, eal]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: dns_tunnel_exfil
    params:
      base_domain: testmynids.org
      chunks: 30
      chunk_size_bytes: 24
      sleep_seconds: 1.0
      encoding: base32
      query_type: A
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/dns-tunnel-live.yml --live
# step-3: Repeat with TXT query type to exercise TXT-burst analytics
cat > /tmp/dns-tunnel-txt.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-NDR-002
name: "NDR validation — DNS tunnel TXT"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - testmynids.org
dry_run: false
tags: [ndr, dns-tunnel, eal]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: dns_tunnel_exfil
    params:
      base_domain: testmynids.org
      chunks: 25
      chunk_size_bytes: 32
      sleep_seconds: 0.5
      encoding: base64
      query_type: TXT
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/dns-tunnel-txt.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Burst of A queries to subdomains of testmynids.org from a single source within a short window.
High-entropy base32/base64 leftmost labels in the query name.
FQDN length well above the host baseline (encoded chunk labels).
TXT query burst to the same zone (data-return channel).

biocs.name: NGFW EAL — long FQDN exceeding host baseline
biocs.description: NGFW EAL — long FQDN exceeding host baseline
biocs.logic:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter app = "dns" or app = "dns-base"
| filter dst_host = "testmynids.org" or dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org"
| alter fqdn_len = len(dns_query_name)
| filter fqdn_len > 60
| fields _time, src_ip, dst_ip, dns_query_name, dns_query_type, fqdn_len

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight: dns_tunnel_exfil plugin exposes expected params schema
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("dns", "dns-base")
| filter dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org"
| comp count() as dns_queries by src_ip, dst_host
| fields src_ip, dst_host, dns_queries

xql_queries.name: NGFW EAL — anomalous DNS query volume from single source
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("dns", "dns-base")
| filter dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org"
| comp count() as query_count, count_distinct(dns_query_name) as unique_labels by src_ip, dst_host
| filter query_count >= 20
| fields src_ip, dst_host, query_count, unique_labels

xql_queries.name: NGFW EAL — high-entropy DNS labels (base32 character profile)
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("dns", "dns-base")
| filter dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org"
| filter dns_query_type = "A"
| alter leftmost_label = arrayindex(split(dns_query_name, "."), 0)
| alter label_len = len(leftmost_label)
| filter label_len >= 16
| comp count() as high_entropy_labels by src_ip, dst_host
| fields src_ip, dst_host, high_entropy_labels

xql_queries.name: NGFW EAL — TXT query burst from single host
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("dns", "dns-base")
| filter dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org"
| filter dns_query_type = "TXT"
| comp count() as txt_query_count by src_ip, dst_host
| filter txt_query_count >= 15
| fields src_ip, dst_host, txt_query_count

xql_queries.name: XSIAM stitching — DNS exfil + endpoint process lineage correlated
xql_queries.query:
preset = network_story
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app in ("dns", "dns-base")
| filter dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org"
| join type=inner (xdr_data
    | filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK
    | fields agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, src_ip as ep_src_ip) as ep ep.ep_src_ip = src_ip
| comp count() as stitched_dns by src_ip, ep.agent_hostname, ep.actor_process_image_name
| fields src_ip, ep.agent_hostname, ep.actor_process_image_name, stitched_dns

correlation_rules.name: DNS Tunneling Exfiltration — Volume Entropy And Length
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(NGFW EAL — long FQDN exceeding host baseline) OR (XQL(NGFW EAL — anomalous DNS query volume from single source) AND XQL(NGFW EAL — high-entropy DNS labels (base32 character profile))) OR XQL(NGFW EAL — TXT query burst from single host)

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Network Security Analytics — DNS Tunneling / Exfiltration
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — High-entropy DNS query name scoring
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Excessive DNS queries to a single domain

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter app = "dns" and dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org" | fields _time, src_ip, dns_query_name, dns_query_type
preset = network_story | filter app = "dns" and dns_query_name contains ".testmynids.org" | comp count() by src_ip
Playbook: sinkhole the abused zone, block at DNS Security, isolate the source endpoint, open exfiltration DFIR ticket.

use_cases.name: DNS Anomaly Detection via NGFW EAL — Tunneling Exfiltration
use_cases.description: Validate that NGFW DNS Security EAL + XSIAM Network Security Analytics detect high-entropy, high-volume, over-baseline-length DNS queries and TXT bursts to a single zone.

test_cases.objective: A-record tunnel burst is detected on volume, entropy and FQDN length.
preconditions: NGFW DNS Security enabled with EAL forwarding to XSIAM.
Recursive resolution for testmynids.org permitted for the simulator pod.
steps: Run SIM-NDR-002 step-02 (base32 A-record tunnel, 30 chunks).
Inspect Network Security Analytics / network_story for the source IP.
success_criteria: XQL `NGFW EAL — anomalous DNS query volume from single source` returns >= 1 row with query_count >= 20.
XQL `NGFW EAL — high-entropy DNS labels (base32 character profile)` returns >= 1 row.
BIOC `NGFW EAL — long FQDN exceeding host baseline` matches the encoded queries.

test_cases.objective: TXT burst is detected and stitched to the originating endpoint.
success_criteria: XQL `NGFW EAL — TXT query burst from single host` returns >= 1 row.
Correlation `CR-NDR-0002` raises one exfiltration incident; XSIAM stitch ties DNS exfil to the endpoint process lineage.

preventive_controls: Enable NGFW DNS Security with tunneling/DGA signatures in block mode.
Force all endpoints to use sanctioned internal resolvers; deny direct outbound DNS (UDP/TCP 53) from clients.
Apply per-zone query-rate limits and block resolution of newly-registered domains.
detection_engineering: Baseline per-host DNS query volume and FQDN length; alert on bursts and over-baseline label lengths.
Score leftmost-label character entropy and base32/base64 profiles per source.
Correlate volume + entropy + length + TXT burst into one exfiltration incident and stitch to endpoint process lineage.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK T1048.003 — Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol

changelog.author: CortexSim generate_card.py
changelog.change: Initial draft auto-generated from scenario SIM-NDR-002.

changelog.author: Henry Reed
